nodes.name: Turdus
nodes.summary: Turdus（Turdus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联91种鸟类，如梅林 (亞瑟王傳說)、Ethiopian thrush、非洲地鸫等91种鸟类。

nodes.name: 博纳维斯塔
nodes.summary: 博纳维斯塔是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括欧乌鸫，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 加那利群岛
nodes.summary: 加那利群岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联13种鸟类，包括赤麻鸭、翎頜鴇、黑腹沙鸡等13种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 农药污染
nodes.summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

nodes.name: 梅林 (亞瑟王傳說)
nodes.summary: 梅林 (亞瑟王傳說)（Turdus merula）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联2种鸟类，如梅林 (亞瑟王傳說)、欧乌鸫。

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 欧乌鸫
nodes.summary: 欧乌鸫（Common blackbird，学名 Turdus merula），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联3个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括农药污染。
nodes.shortSummary: 欧乌鸫（學名：Turdus merula） 又称欧亚乌鸫，是鶇屬的一種鸟类。其下有多個亞種，分佈與歐、亞、非和北美洲，是瑞典的國鳥。後來又被引入澳大利亞和新西蘭，并成為了澳大利亞當地的入侵物種。欧乌鸫是一種留鳥，但有些地方的亞種也會進行遷徙。 欧乌鸫是一種雜食動物，以昆蟲和漿果…
locations: List of birds of North America

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The common blackbird breeds in temperate Eurasia, North Africa, the Canary Islands, and South Asia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: maximus breeding at and remaining above even in winter. This widespread species has been recorded as a vagrant in many locations outside of its normal range in Eurasia, but records from North America are usually thought to be of escaped birds, such as the bird recorded in Quebec in 1971. However, a 1994 record from Bonavista, Newfoundland, has been accepted as a genuine wild bird, and the species is therefore on the North American list.{{cite web|title= The A.O.U

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: maximus breeding at and remaining above even in winter. This widespread species has been recorded as a vagrant in many locations outside of its normal range in Eurasia, but records from North America are usually thought to be of escaped birds, such as the bird recorded in Quebec in 1971. However, a 1994 record from Bonavista, Newfoundland, has been accepted as a genuine wild bird, and the species is therefore on the North American list.{{cite web|title= The A.O.U

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The species is not believed to approach the thresholds for the population decline criterion of the IUCN Red List (i.e., a decline of more than 30% in ten years or three generations), and is therefore categorised as least concern. In the western Palearctic, populations are generally stable or increasing, but there have been local declines, especially on farmland, which may be due to agricultural policies that encouraged farmers to remove hedgerows (which provide nesting places), and to drain damp grassland and increase the use of pesticides, both of which could have reduced the availability of invertebrate food. The common blackbird was introduced to Australia by a bird dealer visiting Melbourne in early 1857, and has since spread from Melbourne and Adelaide to include all of south-eastern Australia, including Tasmania and the Bass Strait islands. The introduced population in Australia is considered a pest because it damages a variety of soft fruits in orchards, parks and gardens, including berries, cherries, stone fruit and grapes.Clarke, G
